creator: swell.k@gmail.com
text:
"Philip M. Gollucci" <pgollucci@p6m7g8.com> writes:

> while here, can we _please_ not update the freaking date?

When file is modified mtime should reflect it.

> This is quite annoying if you revision control these files or if you
> have puppet trigger deltas b/c of that.

VCS shouldn't touch files if size and/or checksum don't differ.

Otherwise, blind restore of mtime can be done like this.

--- b.diff begins here ---
Index: lang/perl5.12/files/use.perl
===================================================================
RCS file: /a/.cvsup/ports/lang/perl5.12/files/use.perl,v
retrieving revision 1.15
diff -u -p -r1.15 use.perl
--- lang/perl5.12/files/use.perl	5 Nov 2009 18:50:45 -0000	1.15
+++ lang/perl5.12/files/use.perl	16 Sep 2010 03:26:01 -0000
@@ -24,11 +24,13 @@ if [ "$2" = "POST-INSTALL" ] ; then
 	need_cleanup_manpath=yes
 	need_spam_make_conf=yes
 	need_spam_manpath=yes
+	need_adjust_mtime=yes
 	need_post_install=yes
 elif [ "$2" = "POST-DEINSTALL" ] ; then
 	need_remove_links=%%LINK_USRBIN%%
 	need_cleanup_make_conf=yes
 	need_cleanup_manpath=yes
+	need_adjust_mtime=yes
 else
 	exit 0;
 fi
@@ -140,6 +144,20 @@ do_spam_manpath()
 	echo " Done."
 }
 
+do_adjust_mtime()
+{
+	echo -n "Adjusting mtime for ${MAKE_CONF}..."
+	if [ -f ${MAKE_CONF}.bak ] ; then
+		/usr/bin/touch -r ${MAKE_CONF}.bak ${MAKE_CONF}
+	fi
+	echo " Done."
+	echo -n "Adjusting mtime for /etc/manpath.config..."
+	if [ -f /etc/manpath.config.bak ] ; then
+		/usr/bin/touch -r /etc/manpath.config.bak /etc/manpath.config
+	fi
+	echo " Done."
+}
+
 [ "$need_remove_links" = "yes" ] && do_remove_links
 [ "$need_create_links" = "yes" ] && do_create_links
 [ "$need_post_install" = "yes" ] && do_post_install
@@ -147,5 +165,6 @@ do_spam_manpath()
 [ "$need_spam_make_conf" = "yes" ] && do_spam_make_conf
 [ "$need_cleanup_manpath" = "yes" ] && do_cleanup_manpath
 [ "$need_spam_manpath" = "yes" ] && do_spam_manpath 
+[ "$need_adjust_mtime" = "yes" ] && do_adjust_mtime
 
 exit 0
--- b.diff ends here ---
